title: coryphantha pallida subsp. calipensis
intro: Coryphantha pallida subsp. calipensis is a rare and endemic subspecies of Coryphantha, a genus of cacti found primarily in the southwestern United States. It is a small to medium-sized cactus with white or pale yellow flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Coryphantha pallida subsp. calipensis can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits including wound healing and immune system support.
